title: thelypteris rurutensis
intro: Thelypteris rurutensis, also known as the Rarotongan fern or Rarotonga maiden's hair fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to the Pacific Islands and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, colds, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Thelypteris rurutensis fern are typically used in teas, infusions, or as a poultice to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
